task_completion.description: Verify that the agent opened the patient record and clinical evaluation for John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GNU Health application with John Zenon's patient file or a clinical evaluation form open.

task_completion.description: Verify that a toxic effect of benzene is documented as an active condition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Diseases/Conditions list or form for John Zenon showing a condition with an ICD-10 code starting with 'T52' (e.g., T52.1) or 'D61' marked as active.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least three hematology-related lab tests were ordered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Lab Requests or Evaluations section showing a minimum of 3 distinct lab test orders (e.g., CBC, erythrocytes, leukocytes, platelets) for John Zenon.

task_completion.description: Verify that a supportive vitamin or hematinic was prescribed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Prescriptions or Medications form for John Zenon showing a prescribed drug whose name includes 'folic', 'vitamin', 'ferrous', 'iron', 'b12', or 'cyanocobalamin'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a follow-up appointment is scheduled between 7 and 14 days from the current date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Appointments section or calendar showing a scheduled appointment for John Zenon with a date that is 7 to 14 days after the system date shown in the screenshots, ideally with a reason mentioning hematology or results review.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show interaction with the GNU Health desktop client windows, menus, and forms, without terminal windows executing SQL scripts or direct database commands.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual GNU Health application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visuals must clearly display the GNU Health interface (Tryton client) rather than a text editor, web browser mockup, or generic notes application.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent entered information into the correct specific fields rather than copy-pasting the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data such as ICD-10 codes, lab names, and medications should be entered into their respective designated form fields or search boxes, not pasted as a single block of text into a general comments or notes field.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Patient: John Zenon. To pass the diagnosis criterion, an active disease record with an ICD-10 code starting with 'T52' or 'D61' must be created. A minimum of 3 new lab orders must be present. The supportive prescription must contain one of the following substrings in its name: folic, vitamin, ferrous, iron, b12, or cyanocobalamin. The follow-up appointment must be scheduled between 7 and 14 days from the task start date.
